modules.name: ApprovalQueue
modules.description: Manager approval queue for pending expenses

modules.name: PolicyManager
modules.description: Configure expense policies and spending limits

POST /api/expenses/ocr.description: Extract expense data from receipt image

events.name: expense:submitted
events.description: Broadcast to approvers when expense submitted

flows.name: Submit Expense
steps: Validate against expense policy (amount, receipt requirement)
Check if amount exceeds approval threshold
If below threshold → auto-approve, notify finance
If above threshold → create approval request, notify manager

flows.name: Approve Expense
steps: If part of report → check if all expenses approved → auto-approve report

flows.name: Receipt OCR
steps: Trigger OCR processing (AWS Textract or similar)
Show OCR result preview for user confirmation

flows.name: Export Expense Report
steps: Fetch report with all expenses and receipts
Generate PDF with itemized list, totals, receipts

computed_fields.name: expense_count
computed_fields.formula: COUNT(expenses) WHERE status != 'rejected' AND deleted_at IS NULL

computed_fields.name: total_this_month
computed_fields.formula: SUM(amount_usd) WHERE expense_date >= first_day_of_month AND submitter_id = current_user

computed_fields.name: policy_violation
computed_fields.formula: amount > policy.max_amount OR (amount > policy.requires_receipt_above AND receipt_url IS NULL)

domain_rules: Expenses can only be edited when in draft status
Receipt required when amount exceeds policy.requires_receipt_above threshold
An expense can only belong to one report at a time
Reports can only be submitted when all contained expenses are in draft status
Approver cannot approve their own expenses
Deleted expenses are excluded from report totals and analytics
Currency conversion uses exchange rate stored at time of expense creation
Policy violations show warnings but do not block submission
generation_rules: All monetary amounts stored as NUMERIC(15,2) in base currency + amount_usd for analytics
Receipt upload goes to S3 with presigned URLs; store only S3 key in DB
Policy violation checks run client-side on form change and server-side on submit
OCR is non-blocking: fire-and-forget then notify via websocket when done
Expense number auto-increments per org: EXP-0001, EXP-0002
Report number auto-increments per org: RPT-0001, RPT-0002
Soft delete only: filter deleted_at IS NULL on all queries
Approval notifications sent via both email and in-app notification
Analytics queries use materialized views refreshed every 15 minutes for performance

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id

external_entity_refs.name: Project
external_entity_refs.description: Project the expense is attributed to.
